medications: Sumatriptan 50 mg as needed for migraines
Naproxen 500 mg as needed for migraine pain (limited use)
Ondansetron ODT 4 mg as needed for migraine-related nausea
Omeprazole 20 mg as needed for reflux
health.mental_health: In recovery from an eating disorder and currently managing work-and-startup stress; attends weekly therapy and uses a relapse-prevention plan (meal reminders, check-ins). Reports occasional anxiety spikes around finances, body image, and sleep disruption triggering migraines.
recent_visits: Primary care visit (Feb 2026): fatigue workup; labs confirmed low ferritin; adjusted iron dosing
Neurology telehealth (Jan 2026): migraine frequency review; renewed sumatriptan and discussed triggers
Therapy session (weekly, ongoing): ED recovery maintenance and stress management
Dental checkup/cleaning with colleague (Dec 2025): routine care; discussed jaw tension from stress

finance.debt: About $14,000 remaining in student loans and ~$4,500 on a 0% intro APR credit card used for business startup costs (portable dental chair, insurance, marketing). Prioritizing paying down the card before the promo ends.
recent_transactions: Purchased portable dental hygiene kit and ultrasonic scaler accessories (~$1,200)
Paid California LLC filing and local business permits (~$170)
Monthly rent payment for shared apartment in Koreatown (~$1,450 share)
Therapy copay and prescriptions (~$85 total in a month)

relationships: Partner: Mateo Rivera (28), freelance videographer; helps film short promo clips for the business
Best friend: Alana Kim (25), ICU nurse at Cedars-Sinai; primary confidant for health recovery and stress
Older sister: Marisol Gutierrez (31) in Riverside; supportive but worries he’s overworking
Mentor: Dr. Priya Shah (dentist employer/clinical lead); advising on ethical boundaries for side business referrals

Uses LinkedIn to connect with local dentists and community clinics.
private_conversations: Discussing relapse fears and body-image triggers with partner and therapist
Negotiating boundaries with employer about marketing the side business without poaching patients
Talking with sister about financial strain and whether to move back home temporarily
Confiding in best friend about migraines affecting work performance and calling out sick

schedule.work_hours: Mon–Thu 8:00am–5:00pm and Fri 8:00am–2:00pm at a dental practice; occasional Saturday morning coverage once a month
regular_appointments: Monthly lab check or follow-up for anemia every 8–12 weeks (morning appointment before work)
Migraine tracking check-in (monthly, self-managed; neurology follow-up every 6 months)
Small business admin block (Sun 10:00am–1:00pm: bookkeeping, content scheduling, client outreach)
upcoming_events: Pop-up oral health screening at a community center in East Hollywood (two Sundays from now)
Meeting with a small-business attorney about client consent forms and HIPAA-adjacent policies (next Tuesday 7:00pm)
Dinner with partner’s family (Friday night) planned around his early shift
schedule.daily_routine: Early wake-up to prep meals/snacks to support recovery, commute to the clinic, then evenings split between light workouts, content creation for the mobile hygiene startup, and admin tasks. Tries to maintain a consistent bedtime because poor sleep increases migraine risk; keeps a strict calendar to prevent overbooking.

professional.company: Private dental practice in Mid-Wilshire (multi-provider general dentistry office)
projects: Leading a periodontal maintenance protocol refresh (new patient education handouts and recall cadence)
Training a newly hired dental assistant on sterilization workflow and room turnover
Building a mobile preventive-care side business focused on oral hygiene coaching and community screenings (non-diagnostic, referral-based)
Developing bilingual (English/Spanish) patient education scripts for post-scaling care
professional.performance_issues: Occasional late arrivals due to migraine mornings and a few requests to swap shifts when therapy or business meetings run long; worried about being perceived as less available while launching the side business. Actively documenting coverage arrangements to avoid impacting patient flow.
workplace_relationships: Strong rapport with Dr. Priya Shah (clinical lead) but careful about conflicts of interest
Friendly but competitive dynamic with another hygienist, Kelsey Nguyen, who also wants prime hygiene blocks
Front-desk manager, Ron Patel, is supportive if scheduling changes are communicated early

hobbies: Beach runs/walks at Santa Monica (low-intensity for stress management)
Photography and short-form video editing for business content
Pick-up basketball at a local rec center
Exploring coffee shops to work on business planning (limited caffeine due to migraines)
habits: Uses a migraine trigger log (sleep, hydration, caffeine, stress)
Sets phone reminders for regular meals/snacks to support ED recovery
Limits alcohol and avoids fasting; keeps electrolyte packets in his work bag
Evening screen-time cutoff when possible to protect sleep
Occasionally uses OTC melatonin (low dose) during high-stress weeks
lifestyle.living_situation: Shares a two-bedroom apartment in Koreatown with a roommate (Luis Mendoza, 27) to keep costs manageable while funding business startup expenses; partner stays over on weekends. Uses the dining table as a temporary workstation for packaging supplies and admin.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Aims for balanced, iron-supportive meals (lean meats, beans, leafy greens) with vitamin C pairings; moderate caffeine and avoids known migraine triggers like skipping meals and excessive energy drinks. Keeps snacks accessible during long clinic days to prevent blood sugar dips.
